events.title: Ebola outbreak follows hunger and displacement crisis in DR Congo
events.description: A day after the World Health Organization (WHO) declared the new Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo an international emergency, top global disease transmission experts stressed that the chances of another global pandemic similar to the 2019 coronavirus emergency are increasing all the time.

events.title: WHO considers use of experimental vaccines as Ebola cases and deaths rise in DRC
events.description: WHO chief said he was ‘deeply concerned’ after at least 500 suspected Ebola cases and 130 deaths reported in outbreak of Bundibugyo strain ‘It’s heartbreaking’: panic in eastern DRC over return of Ebola Global health leaders are considering whether vaccines or medicines still in development could be used to fight Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, as the World Health Organization’s chief said he was deeply concerned by the outbreak’s speed and scale. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said there had been at least 500 suspected cases of Ebola and 130 suspected deaths in DRC since the new outbreak began – up from about 200 cases and 65 deaths when it was announced on Friday . Continue reading...

events.title: Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a ‘Public Health Emergency of International Concern’
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, citing rising cases, cross-border spread and significant uncertainties about the scale of the epidemic.

events.title: 1.1 billion people live in slums. Can they be housed in dignity?
events.description: Ministers, mayors, international organisations, urban planners and experts are in Baku, Azerbaijan, to tackle one of the world’s fastest-growing challenges: the global housing crisis, which, according to the United Nations, affects nearly 2.8 billion people worldwide.

events.title: World News in Brief: Geopolitical tensions impact global economy, Gaza aid funding shortfall, violence in South Sudan, UN condemns deadly mosque attack
events.description: The global economy is entering a more fragile period as geopolitical conflicts, rising energy costs and financial instability threaten global growth and trade.

events.title: Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows
events.description: Rising volume of components imported from China prompts warning of cannibalisation of European industries Europe is facing a fresh China shock that threatens to cannibalise local factories, leading to job losses and de facto colonisation of industry by Beijing, trade analysts and representatives have said. They fear the plunging exchange rate and support for Chinese “zombie firms” has echoes of the crisis in the US 25 years ago when the term “China shock” was coined. It referred to the impact of China bursting on to the global trade stage after becoming a member of the World Trade Organization, with soaring imports displacing local industries and causing the loss of up to 2.5m jobs. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows

events.title: WHO evaluates vaccines as efforts ramp up to control Ebola outbreak
events.description: The WHO said Tuesday it was examining whether any candidate vaccines or treatments could be used to rein in what it warned could be a lengthy Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The WHO has declared the surge of the highly contagious haemorrhagic fever, suspected to have killed 131 people and infected more than 500, an international health emergency.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO evaluates vaccines as efforts ramp up to control Ebola outbreak

events.title: WHO says risk of global Ebola spread is low, but high at national, regional levels
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday said the Ebola epidemic risk was high at the national and regional levels and low at the global level.

events.title: Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean
events.description: Record-breaking temperatures, deadly floods, worsening drought and intensifying hurricanes are placing millions of people across Latin America and the Caribbean at growing risk of hunger, displacement and water shortages, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean

events.title: Race to contain ebola outbreak in war-torn DRC where weak infrastructure hampers response
events.description: Annette Young welcomes Julie Drouet, DRC Director for Action Against Hunger. She offers a sobering assessment of the early Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Speaking from the frontline of a rapidly evolving health emergency, Drouet describes a humanitarian system racing against time in one of the world’s most fragile and conflict-affected regions. They are not only facing epidemiological challenges of containing Ebola, but also the deeper structural vulnerabilities that make outbreaks in eastern DRC uniquely perilous.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Race to contain ebola outbreak in war-torn DRC where weak infrastructure hampers response

events.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a public health emergency after 80 suspected deaths
events.description: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda an international public health emergency after dozens of suspected deaths were reported.

events.title: How the Hormuz crisis keeps disrupting kitchens, ports and paychecks
events.description: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran may have eased fears of a wider regional war, but persistent instability around the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global trade, drive up energy costs and fuel a growing jobs and cost-of-living crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How the Hormuz crisis keeps disrupting kitchens, ports and paychecks

events.title: Thailand tightens visa rules for tourists, citing crime by foreigners
events.description: Move brings an end to a 60 day visa-free stay that was agreed with 93 countries, including the UK, US and much of Europe Thailand is drastically cutting the length of visa-free stays for tourists from more than 90 countries in an effort to curb crime involving foreign nationals, officials said on Tuesday. Tourism is a crucial source of jobs in Thailand, and the country has struggled to return to the number of foreign arrivals recorded before the Covid pandemic. However, concerns over visitors breaking the law have led to calls for tougher immigration rules Continue reading...

events.title: WHO head 'deeply concerned' over 'scale and speed' of Ebola spread, says emergency committee will meet
events.description: WHO chief convenes emergency committee over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, citing over 500 suspected cases and 131 fatalities from the virus.

events.title: World News in Brief: More attacks in Ukraine, violence against children in Haiti, refugee IDs in Africa
events.description: Civilians, including humanitarians, continue to face great danger across war-torn Ukraine amid ongoing hostilities, according to the UN humanitarian relief coordination office there, OCHA.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: More attacks in Ukraine, violence against children in Haiti, refugee IDs in Africa

events.title: Australia faces 'biggest diphtheria outbreak' in decades
events.description: Australia is likely facing its "biggest diphtheria outbreak" in decades, the country's health minister has warned, as an investigation continues into a possible death.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia faces 'biggest diphtheria outbreak' in decades

events.title: Lebanon accuses Iran of inserting IRGC terrorists into country 'under guise of diplomatic activity'
events.description: Lebanon reportedly filed a precedent-setting UN complaint against Iran amid U.S.-facilitated Israel-Lebanon normalization talks in Washington, D.C.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lebanon accuses Iran of inserting IRGC terrorists into country 'under guise of diplomatic activity'

events.title: Iran warns of war ‘beyond the region’ if US resumes attacks
events.description: Iran has warned the United States against resuming attacks, threatening to open new fronts if the current ceasefire ends following remarks from Donald Trump that the US may have to deliver another “big hit” against Tehran. Meanwhile, Iranian civilians are preparing for a new flare-up, as negotiations have yet to lead to any concrete decisions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran warns of war ‘beyond the region’ if US resumes attacks

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Concerns grow over nuclear safety after UAE drone strike
events.description: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session in New York to discuss the situation in the Middle East following reports of a drone strike near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the United Arab Emirates. IAEA Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi briefed members as concerns grow over nuclear safety and security in the region. Follow our live coverage below for updates and for coverage of all key meetings at UN Headquarters, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Concerns grow over nuclear safety after UAE drone strike

events.title: New Ebola outbreak leaves 65 dead as officials warn of cross-border spread
events.description: A new Ebola outbreak in Congo has killed 65 people with 246 suspected cases as officials investigate which strain is driving the deadly spread.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Ebola outbreak leaves 65 dead as officials warn of cross-border spread

events.title: Iran ‘prefers diplomacy’ but if there’s a war, Tehran says it’s ready
events.description: Iran and the US traded threats as President Donald Trump warned of renewed strikes a day after he said he held off a major assault in hopes of a peace deal. Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says Iranian officials have maintained their position that “they prefer diplomacy. They don't see a military solution to this conflict,” he explains. “But if there's a war, they're ready for it.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran ‘prefers diplomacy’ but if there’s a war, Tehran says it’s ready

events.title: 28 killed in a drone strike on a market in Sudan’s West Kordofan, rights group says
events.description: A local rights group says a drone strike on a market in central Sudan has killed 28 people and wounded dozens more
events.ticker_label: 🪖 28 killed in a drone strike on a market in Sudan’s West Kordofan, rights group says

events.title: Nuclear terror threat ‘has never been so high’
events.description: The widespread availability of new technology, such as militarised drones and artificial intelligence, means that the current threat of nuclear terrorism is higher than it has ever been.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nuclear terror threat ‘has never been so high’

events.title: As WHO sounds alarm over Ebola in DRC, what can be learned from previous outbreaks?
events.description: Conflict, mistrust and delayed detection could complicate response to emergency caused by Bundibugyo variant To be around the centre of an Ebola outbreak is to become used to the smell of chlorine. At hospitals and government buildings, surfaces are sprayed with it and hands washed in a 0.05% solution that can kill the virus in 60 seconds. Infrared handheld thermometers take temperatures at airports and border crossings. Any indication of a fever prevents passage. Contact-tracing teams crisscross the countryside. Continue reading...

events.title: WHO chief concerned over 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
events.description: The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.

events.title: Housing crisis takes centre stage at World Urban Forum in Baku
events.description: Now is the time to place housing at the heart of sustainable development. That was the message delivered on Monday by UN Secretary General António Guterres to participants at a global forum on urban sustainability under way this week in Baku.

events.title: More die of suspected Ebola as WHO warns that numbers will rise further
events.description: In its latest update, the World Health Organization says there have now been 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 More die of suspected Ebola as WHO warns that numbers will rise further

events.title: Woman shot dead by police in Jamaica at protest over previous police shooting
events.description: Investigation launched as video circulates online showing officer firing at vehicle and colleagues dragging away body Authorities in Jamaica have launched an investigation after CCTV footage of a woman’s fatal shooting by police sent shock waves across the Caribbean nation. Footage circulating on social media shows a police officer firing at a vehicle during a protest on Sunday in Granville, in Jamaica’s north-western parish of St James. The bullet hit Latoya Bulgin, 45, who was behind the wheel of the vehicle. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Woman shot dead by police in Jamaica at protest over previous police shooting

events.title: Arsenal clinch Premier League title after 22-year drought
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, May 20, 2026: The 17th Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo is getting a lot of coverage. Papers are looking into the impact of recent aid cuts on the emergency response plan. In the United States, President Donald Trump's battle with the Internal Revenue Service continues to make headlines. Also, The New York Times discusses Trump's World Cup delegate, Andrew Giuliani. Finally, the Gunners are back on top, as Arsenal win their 14th Premier League title.

events.title: Father-of-eight killed in San Diego mosque shooting hailed as hero
events.description: Security guard Amin Abdullah, one of three killed in the attack, was described as "a shining light".
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Father-of-eight killed in San Diego mosque shooting hailed as hero

events.title: San Diego mosque shooters radicalized online, says FBI
events.description: Two teenagers who shot and killed three people in an attack on a San Diego mosque were radicalized online where they first met and shared white supremacist views, according to authorities and writings they authored. The 17- and 18-year-old assailants took their own lives shortly after Monday's shooting.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 San Diego mosque shooters radicalized online, says FBI

events.title: UN underscores protection of nuclear sites following drone strike on Emirati plant

events.title: Report finds global aid system is failing to address today's humanitarian crises

events.title: 'Taiwan Travelogue' wins the 2026 International Booker Prize
events.description: The novel is the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award, which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.

events.title: Rising Cuba-US tensions amid talk of drones, emergency plans
events.description: Cuba’s reported purchase of drones and renewed threats with the US are stoking fears of escalation. Amid blackouts and shortages, many Cubans expressed outrage at official advice to prepare for potential attack.

events.title: Opposition leaders in the Bahamas demand probe after US ties politician to drug trafficking case
events.description: Opposition leaders in the Bahamas are demanding an investigation after a U.S. federal agent alleged that a suspected drug trafficker who survived a recent plane crash near Florida was rescued and found with roughly $30,000

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Virginia teacher shot by 6-year-old says boy showed ‘violent’ warning signs – assistant principal now on trial

events.title: Blast outside a Syrian defense ministry building kills a soldier, wounds 12 people

events.title: WHO assembly opens under shadow of Ebola, hantavirus and funding cuts
events.description: From Ebola outbreaks in Central Africa to the complex evacuation of passengers aboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) opened its annual assembly on Monday as countries grapple with disease outbreaks, deep funding cuts and growing geopolitical tension.

events.title: ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst

events.title: Officials to meet Australians detained by Israel – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Labor denies CGT reform will ‘kill startups’ as tech giant Canva warns of stifling innovation Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The NSW economy will grow less than expected next financial year amid rising inflation and the global oil shock, the state’s treasurer will warn ahead of next month’s budget. In a speech today ahead of the 23 June state budget, Daniel Mookhey will warn that NSW will only avoid a recession in 2026-27 because of the number of renewable energy projects now under construction in the state. Attentive budget watchers will notice a slowdown more pronounced in NSW than elsewhere in the federation. The simple reason is that higher inflation has led to higher interest rates which is lowering consumption spending; the point of the [Reserve Bank of Australia’s] increased interest rates. But the more complicated explanation is this: higher interest rates hurt working Australians in every state, but they hurt working Australians more in this state. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Officials to meet Australians detained by Israel – as it happened

events.title: Middle East: Iran says any new attack will widen war
events.description: Iran's army has threatened to "open new fronts" in Tehran's war with the US and Israel in the event of new attacks. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East: Iran says any new attack will widen war

events.title: Los Angeles World Cup workers vow strike if ICE deployed at stadium
events.description: Workers at Los Angeles' SoFi Stadium vowed Monday to go on strike if federal immigration enforcement agents are deployed at the venue when it hosts World Cup matches next month. The UNITE HERE Local 11, representing around 2,000 hospitality employees, is demanding federal guarantees that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) will not be used in any of the eight World Cup matches scheduled to take place at the stadium.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Los Angeles World Cup workers vow strike if ICE deployed at stadium

events.title: Mango clothing tycoon’s son named suspect in death of billionaire whose brand stretches across US
events.description: The son of billionaire Mango founder Isak Andic was arrested in Spain amid a homicide investigation into his father's fatal mountain fall.

events.title: Rubio criticizes WHO’s Ebola response as US continues sweeping public health cuts
events.description: US secretary of state says WHO was ‘a little late’ in identifying deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said on Tuesday that the World Health Organization (WHO) was “a little late” in identifying the deadly Ebola outbreak in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. On Tuesday, Rubio told reporters: “The lead is obviously going to be CDC [Centers for Disease Control] and the World Health Organization, which was a little late to identify this thing unfortunately.” Continue reading...

events.title: ‘US imperialism’: Anger in Philippines as war with Iran sends oil prices soaring
events.description: As Washington continues to struggle to reach an agreement with Iran that would permanently open the Strait of Hormuz, the impact of its closure is still being felt around the world, not least in the Philippines, where rising oil prices have pushed many to breaking point and anger, mostly directed to the US, has spilled into the streets. From Manila, our correspondent Jan Camenzind Broomby reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘US imperialism’: Anger in Philippines as war with Iran sends oil prices soaring

events.title: EU makes headway towards ratifying US trade deal, under pressure from Trump
events.description: After marathon five-hour talks, representatives from the EU Parliament and the EU Council agreed on Wednesday to go ahead and ratify the trade pact found with the US in July 2025. US President Donald Trump had threatened to increase tariffs on EU autos to 25 percent if the deal wasn't implemented by July 4. Also in this edition, talks between Samsung and its workers went down to the wire ahead of a scheduled 18-day strike on Thursday.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.description: Canadian national Sunny Manhertz allegedly voted illegally in federal and state elections from 2008 to 2024 while living in Massachusetts as a lawful permanent resident.

events.title: Did Trump really rescue Venezuela? – podcast
events.description: Tom Phillips on life in the country four months after the US abduction of the former president Nicolás Maduro “The last time I flew out of Venezuela was right at the start of August 2024, just after the disputed presidential election,” the Guardian’s Latin America correspondent, Tom Phillips , tells Annie Kelly . “It was a moment of real turmoil. There was a huge wave of repression that was unfolding as Nicolás Maduro tried to silence any kind of dissent to his bogus claim to have won that election. Thousands were thrown in prison, many were going underground, and journalists were racing to get out of the country.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Did Trump really rescue Venezuela? – podcast

events.title: 'Crony capitalism and oligarchy': 'Remarkable correlation' between Adani's rise & Modi’s ascendancy
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Paranjoy Guha-Thakurta, Indian journalist, investigative author and filmmaker. He offers a sweeping critique of the nexus between oligarchic wealth, political power, and weakened institutional accountability in contemporary India. Drawing on the allegations surrounding billionaire industrialist Gautam Adani, U.S. sanctions investigations, and the politics of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, he argues that “crony capitalism and oligarchy” have become deeply embedded within the fabric of the Indian state.

events.title: DRC Ebola outbreak: hundreds of suspected cases, no vaccine
events.description: A fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has health workers rushing to stop transmission while the roll out of any potential vaccine is months away, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

events.title: Court names Mango tycoon's son as suspect in probe into father's death
events.description: The son of Isak Andic, founder of fashion group Mango, was named by a judge in Barcelona on Tuesday as a suspect in an investigation into his father's death in a 2024 cliff-top fall. Jonathan Andic, 45, was arrested ⁠and questioned by police in Catalonia, before later being released after posting 1 million euros in bail. France 24’s correspondent in Madrid Sarah Morris has the latest.

events.title: Mango founder's son arrested in investigation into father's death

events.title: ‘Vein of racism’: race discrimination commissioner accuses One Nation and Coalition of scapegoating immigrants

The state’s former security minister Gerardo Mérida Sánchez crossed the border into Arizona last week and was taken into custody by US marshals, Mexico’s security ministry said. Sinaloa’s former finance minister, Enrique Díaz Vega, was taken into custody in New York. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘How are we going to survive this?’ Wellington faces six-month wait to halt sewage spill
events.description: Millions of litres of sewage have been spilling into the capital city’s waters since February after the catastrophic failure of a Moa Point wastewater plant A fix to stop millions of litres of sewage continuing to pour into the waters off the coast of New Zealand’s capital, Wellington will be in place by November, officials have said, with full repairs at the cost of NZ$53.5m by late next year. More than 100 days since the catastrophic failure of the city’s wastewater treatment plant on 4 February, a mix of raw and partially screened human effluent is still being flushed directly into the Pacific Ocean. events.title: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit

When Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday, many feared the unpredictable US leader could upend Washington’s longstanding support for Taipei. But beyond a starkly worded statement from Xi stressing China’s claims over Taiwan, which it claims as part of its territory despite never having ruled it, initial signs appeared good for Taipei. events.title: From favelas to informal settlements, new UN report points to housing solutions that work
events.description: From Thailand to Jordan, from Brazil to Germany, new approaches to housing are quietly taking shape. Residents of informal settlements once facing eviction are rebuilding their communities with State support. Refugees and host communities are reclaiming neglected spaces, turning them greener and safer. In Brazil, favelas are being upgraded rather than torn down, while in Germany, rent controls are helping to steady the market.

The review of FoI administration found there was no consistent, transparent or accountable decision-making around requests and the pro-disclosure objectives of the law were being flouted. events.title: Lax rules and rise in for-profit childcare allow predators to abuse children, NSW inquiry finds

In its final report, published on Wednesday, a NSW upper house inquiry into the Early Childhood Education and Care (ECEC) sector found “the proliferation of for-profit services and a lax regulatory approach” had led to “predators” being allowed to work in childcare and abuse children. events.title: Enhanced Games: Pushing human potential or lethal circus?
events.description: Investors like Peter Thiel and Donald Trump Jr. see the Enhanced Games as proof of how far human limits can stretch. For doping experts, the danger is clear and grave. DW explains the latest controversial sporting event.

events.title: Millions follow India's viral 'cockroach' movement
events.description: What started as an internet joke is fast becoming young India's newest symbol of resilience, survival and collective frustration. Meanwhile, PM Modi has reached Italy for the last leg of his tour. DW has more.

events.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping
events.description: Presidents Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin each praised their countries' bilateral relations during the Russian leader's visit to Beijing. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Taiwan's Lai rejects China pressure, pledges stronger defense
events.description: Lai Ching-te defended Taiwan's sovereignty after US President Donald Trump suggested that arms sales to the island could be used as leverage in talks with China.

events.title: US to reduce troops in Europe to 2021 levels, Pentagon says
events.description: The scaling back would bring the number of US troops on the continent to the level before Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The Pentagon said that was in line with Trump's 'America first' agenda.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US to reduce troops in Europe to 2021 levels, Pentagon says

events.title: US troop withdrawal from Europe to take 'years' — general
events.description: NATO top commander Alexus Grynkewich said the US withdrawal would be "well-synchronized" with European allies. It comes after Donald Trump announced a decision to pull 5,000 troops out of Germany.

events.title: $181.2 million Pollock shatters auction record at Christie's
events.description: The work from the collection of publishing magnate S.I. Newhouse led auction week at Christie's in New York. The canvas was included in a billion dollar sale.

events.title: Pioneer of pop music: Cher turns 80
events.description: Cher has had countless hits and sparked more than a few controversies — while always doing things her own way. At 80, she remains as iconic as ever.

events.title: Germany beefs up civil defense
events.description: Germany's government wants to spend €10 billion to better protect its population in case of emergencies. The backlog is huge.

events.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
events.description: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

events.title: Energy in spotlight as Putin visits China
events.description: President Xi Jinping welcomed president Putin to Beijing on Wednesday for a bilateral summit set to last two days. In his opening remarks Xi stressed the need for the cessation of hostilities in the Middle East to stablise energy supplies and the pair discussed the "Power of Siberia 2" natural gas pipeline, although no deal on it was reached.

events.title: Woman dies after falling into manhole
events.description: A woman has died after falling into a manhole in New York.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Woman dies after falling into manhole

events.title: UK waters down Russian oil sanctions
events.description: The UK government has watered-down sanctions on Russia, allowing diesel and jet fuel from Russian crude oil to enter the UK if it is refined in other countries.

events.title: Bodies of divers who died exploring Maldives cave recovered
events.description: The bodies of two Italian divers who died while exploring a cave in the Maldives have been recovered.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bodies of divers who died exploring Maldives cave recovered

events.title: Ukraine ally Britain eases sanctions on Russian oil as fuel prices surge
events.description: The U.K. government has quietly eased some sanctions on Russian oil to help Britons cope with rising costs

events.title: President Trump’s portrait hits New Delhi traffic as US Embassy rolls out rickshaws
events.description: In New Delhi, some auto-rickshaws are now displaying images of U_S_ President Donald Trump with the slogan “Happy Birthday America!”

events.title: Taiwan's Lai says he would tell Trump he hopes to continue arm purchases
events.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te says that if given the chance he would tell U.S. President Donald Trump he hopes to continue U.S. arms purchases that are essential for peace

events.title: Putin and Xi hail their high-level ties and growing energy trade as they meet in Beijing
events.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin have hailed their strategic ties and energy trade during a meeting in Beijing

events.title: UK police investigate allegations of historic child sex abuse following Epstein file release
events.description: Police in the UK are investigating two allegations of historic child sex abuse linked to the Epstein files

events.title: Turning silence into stories: Cambodian activist amplifies LGBTQI+ youth voices
events.description: Cambodian activist Panha Theng, a 2025 UN Young Leader for the Sustainable Development Goals, says visibility and safe spaces remain critical for LGBTQI+ youth across Southeast Asia amid continuing stigma and discrimination.

events.title: Man accused of killing partner arrested in Mexico nearly two years after fleeing with their two children
events.description: Camron Lee was arrested in Mexico in connection with the 2024 murder of his partner, Angelica Bravo, after allegedly fleeing with their two children, the FBI says.

events.title: Pentagon cuts Brigade Combat Teams in Europe as Trump pressures NATO on spending
events.description: The Pentagon announced it is reducing Brigade Combat Teams in Europe from four to three as the Trump administration pressures NATO allies on defense.

events.title: California doctor convicted of $45M Medicare fraud scheme that funded luxury vacations and a $12K crossbow
events.description: California doctor accused of submitting $45 million in false Medicare claims for Botox injections was convicted of wire fraud and obstruction charges.

events.title: Second batch of UFO files set to be released after lawmaker teased ‘Holy Crap’ moment
events.description: The Trump administration is expected to release a second wave of declassified UAP files very soon as part of its PURSUE transparency program.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Second batch of UFO files set to be released after lawmaker teased ‘Holy Crap’ moment

events.title: New York's 'wrong-headed' defendant-friendly laws helped toss Mangione evidence: retired cop
events.description: Judge ruled Pennsylvania police conducted an unconstitutional search of Luigi Mangione's backpack, tossing key evidence in the murder case against him.

events.title: Tyler Robinson's defense team fights to seal evidence and punish prosecutors in Charlie Kirk's assassination
events.description: Tyler Robinson, accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk at a Utah event, faces court hearing on defense motions to seal evidence and close testimony.

events.title: Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died
events.description: Uyghur mother Mihrigul Tursun recounts years of alleged detention, torture and family separation in China's Xinjiang camps in emotional testimony.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died
